Functions for starting and handling operations in accessory manufacturing mode.

# Purpose
This C header file declares two functions related to an accessory manufacturing mode. The function [`accessory_mfg_mode_start`](<#accessory_mfg_mode_start>) is intended to be called at the beginning of the manufacturing process to perform initial setup tasks. The function [`accessory_mfg_mode_handle_char`](<#accessory_mfg_mode_handle_char>) is designed to be called within an Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) to process a character received from an accessory connector, returning a boolean value to indicate success or failure. The file includes the `stdbool.h` library to utilize the `bool` data type.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### accessory\_mfg\_mode\_start<!-- {{#callable_declaration:accessory_mfg_mode_start}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/prf/accessory/accessory_mfg_mode.h#L22>)

Starts the manufacturing mode setup process.
- **Description**: Use this function to initiate the setup process when entering manufacturing mode. It is important to call this function at the beginning of the manufacturing mode to ensure proper initialization. If the `DISABLE_PROMPT` preprocessor directive is defined, the function will return immediately without performing any actions.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`accessory_mfg_mode_start`](<accessory_mfg_mode.c.md#accessory_mfg_mode_start>)  (Implementation)


---
### accessory\_mfg\_mode\_handle\_char<!-- {{#callable_declaration:accessory_mfg_mode_handle_char}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/prf/accessory/accessory_mfg_mode.h#L25>)

Handles a character input from the accessory connector during manufacturing mode.
- **Description**: Use this function to process a character received from the accessory connector while in manufacturing mode. It must be called within an interrupt service routine (ISR) context. The function processes printable ASCII characters by appending them to a prompt context. It handles the Enter key by scheduling a command execution and the CTRL-C key by resetting the prompt context. The function returns a boolean indicating whether a context switch should occur.
- **Inputs**:
    - `c`: A character from the accessory connector. Valid values are ASCII characters. Printable characters (0x20 to 126) are appended to the prompt context. Special handling occurs for the Enter key (0x0d) and CTRL-C (0x03). Other values are ignored. The function does not handle invalid input explicitly.
- **Output**: Returns a boolean indicating if a context switch should occur after processing the character.
- **See Also**: [`accessory_mfg_mode_handle_char`](<accessory_mfg_mode.c.md#accessory_mfg_mode_handle_char>)  (Implementation)
